mnemonics: COMPETE: Competitive Outcomes Maximize Price Equilibrium Through Equilibria
keyPoints: Competitive games model markets with many participants where no single player can influence the outcome (price takers)
The fundamental theorem states that competitive equilibria are exactly the outcomes that can be achieved by suitable cash transfers in the game
These games demonstrate the efficiency of market equilibria and provide a game-theoretic foundation for competitive markets
commonMistakes: Confusing competitive games (price takers) with oligopoly/market games (price makers)
Assuming competitive equilibria always exist without meeting required conditions like convex preferences
Forgetting that the competitive equilibrium must simultaneously clear all markets in the economy
